template.name: Outbound — 3-Step Warm/Triggered Cadence
template.description: Signal-driven warm outbound variant of outbound-3-step-cadence-cold. Attaches to a signal-monitor (job-change, hiring, funding) and inherits the signal context into the first line. Tighter Sending Gate (signal severity = HOT or WARM required); explicit signal_context block fed into Claygent for first-line generation.
template.estimated_cost_per_row: 10-14 credits per signal-triggered contact at Claygent layer
template.expected_match_rate: Sending Gate pass rate: 70-85% (warm signals are pre-qualified upstream); Read-Out-Loud pass on first lines: target 19/20
required_resources: Email enrichment for the new contact context

columns.name: signal_type
columns.notes: job_change / hiring / funding / leadership / product_launch / tech_adoption

columns.name: signal_payload
columns.notes: Original signal data — fed into Claygent first-line prompt

columns.name: sending_gate_eligible
columns.formula_text: AND(email IS NOT NULL, email_verified_status IN ["valid", "accept_all_high"], suppression_check == FALSE, signal_severity IN ["HOT", "WARM"], signal_recency_days <= {{MAX_SIGNAL_AGE_DAYS}})

columns.name: signal_context_summary
first_line
columns.claygent_prompt:
ROLE: Peer-to-peer B2B sales rep writing the OPENING line of a triggered outbound email.

CONTEXT:
- Recipient: {first_name}, {title} at {company_name}
- Signal: {signal_context_summary.summary}
- Angle: {signal_context_summary.angle}
- Timing proof: {signal_context_summary.timing_proof}

VOICE: {{VOICE_DESCRIPTOR}}

TASK:
Write ONE sentence (15-25 words) that:
- Leads with the signal observation directly — congratulations / saw your move / etc. (warm-tone OK because signal is recent)
- Names the timing proof specifically
- Sets up the relevance of YOUR product to THEIR new situation

Banned words: unlock, leverage, supercharge, hope
Banned starts: "I saw", "I noticed", "Hope"

FORMAT:
Return the sentence ONLY.

FALLBACK:
Return: "[signal context unclear]" — will skip step 1.
columns.notes: Warm tone is OK here, unlike cold cadence. Tone calibration is different.

columns.name: subject_line
columns.claygent_prompt:
ROLE: B2B subject line writer. 3-6 words.

CONTEXT:
- Recipient: {first_name}
- Signal angle: {signal_context_summary.angle}

TASK:
Write a 3-6 word subject. Reference the signal lightly. One of these patterns:
- "Congrats on {signal_event_short}"
- "{signal_event_short} → {first_name}"
- "{first_name} - {one-word context}"

FORMAT: Subject line only.

columns.name: step_1_eligible
columns.formula_text: AND(sending_gate_eligible == TRUE, LENGTH(first_line) >= 30, NOT(first_line CONTAINS "[signal context unclear]"))

columns.name: step_2_eligible
columns.formula_text: AND(step_1_eligible == TRUE, step_1_sent_at IS NOT NULL, step_1_replied != TRUE, step_1_bounced != TRUE, DAYS_SINCE(step_1_sent_at) >= 2, DAYS_SINCE(step_1_sent_at) <= 7, linkedin_url IS NOT NULL)
columns.notes: Tighter window than cold (2-7 days vs 3-10) because signal freshness decays fast

columns.name: step_3_eligible
columns.formula_text: AND(step_2_eligible == TRUE OR step_1_eligible == TRUE, step_1_replied != TRUE, step_1_bounced != TRUE, DAYS_SINCE(step_1_sent_at) >= 7, DAYS_SINCE(step_1_sent_at) <= 14)

actions.name: sequencer_push_step_1_warm
actions.notes: Different cadence for WARM — softer follow-up cadence

verification_checklist: 5-row sample: signal context summarizes correctly (no JSON-as-text bugs)
First lines reference the signal explicitly (not generic)
Read-Out-Loud test: 19/20 first lines pass (warm cadences hold higher bar)
HOT vs WARM cadence routing works — different cadence IDs receive correct severity
Step 2 window (2-7 days) is tighter than cold cadence — verify in sequencer
Signal recency gate: a 60-day-old signal correctly classifies as NOT eligible (assuming MAX_SIGNAL_AGE_DAYS = 30)
